RESPONSE TO STP. STATEMENT OF DISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN OPPOS. TO MTN FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST VIKING SANDBLASTING REGARDING THE DUTY TO DEFEND FILED BY DEFENDANT VAN ACKER CONSTRUCTION ASSOCIATES, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-COMPLAINANT VAN ACKER CONSTRUCTION ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST VIKING SANDBLASTING RE THE DUTY TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. (CCP 437C(F).) CROSS-COMPLAINANT VAN ACKER CONSTRUCTION ASSOCIATES, INC. ("VAN ACKER") HAS MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT PERTAINING TO CROSS-DEFENDANT VIKING SANDBLASTING ("VIKING") DUTY TO DEFEND UNDER THE SUBCONTRACT. "THE PERSON INDEMNIFYING IS BOUND, ON REQUEST OF THE PERSON INDEMNIFIED, TO DEFEND ACTIONS OR PROCEEDINGS BROUGHT AGAINST THE LATTER IN RESPECT TO THE MATTERS EMBRACED BY THE INDEMNITY, BUT THE PERSON INDEMNIFIED HAS THE RIGHT TO CONDUCT SUCH DEFENSES, IF HE CHOOSES TO DO SO." (CCP 2778(F).) IN THIS CASE, THE INDEMNITY PROVISION PROVIDES THE DUTY TO DEFEND. (UMF NO. 8; CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S EVIDENCE, EXHIBIT H.) THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE CONNECTED TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE SUBCONTRACT, NAMELY THE SANDBLASTING ACTIVITIES. (COMPLAINT, 7.) IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT VAN ACKER TENDERED THE DEFENSE, AND VIKING HAS NOT ACCEPTED TENDER. (UMF NOS. 10, 11.) IN TURN, VIKING HAS NOT MET ITS SHIFTING BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE COLLATERAL SOURCE RULE DOES NOT APPLY IN THIS INSTANCE AS VAN ACKER SEEKS A RULING ON THE DUTY TO DEFEND, AND NOT REIMBURSEMENT OF ATTORNEY FEES. THIS IS FACTUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE FROM BRAMALEA CALIFORNIA, INC. V. RELIABLE INTERIORS, INC. (2004) 119 CAL.APP.4TH 468, 472. THE INDEMNITY PROVISION IN THE SUBCONTRACT IS CLEAR AND EXPLICIT, AND VIKING HAD A DUTY TO DEFEND FROM TENDER. "A CONTRACTUAL PROMISE TO "DEFEND" ANOTHER AGAINST SPECIFIED CLAIMS CLEARLY CONNOTES AN OBLIGATION OF ACTIVE RESPONSIBILITY, FROM THE OUTSET, FOR THE PROMISEE'S DEFENSE AGAINST SUCH CLAIMS." (CRAWFORD V. WEATHER SHIELD MFG. INC. (2008) 44 CAL.4TH 541, 553 - 554.) WHETHER VAN ACKER WAS NEGLIGENT IS NOT AT ISSUE IN THE DUTY TO DEFEN
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANTS VAN ACKER CONSTRUCTION ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE: VIKING SANDBLASTING, INC.'S DUTY TO DEFEND

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-26-2014 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Viking Sandblasting Re The Duty To Defend
Off Calendar Apr-15-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-COMPLAINANT VAN ACKER CONSTRUCTION ASSOCIATES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Viking Sandblasting Re The Duty To Defend is Granted. (CCP 437c(f).) Cross-Complainant Van Acker Construction Associates, Inc. ("Van Acker") has met its initial burden to demonstrate that there are no triable issues of fact pertaining to Cross-Defendant Viking Sandblasting ("Viking") duty to defend under the subcontract. "The person indemnifying is bound, on request of the person indemnified, to defend actions or proceedings brought against the latter in respect to the matters embraced by the indemnity, but the person indemnified has the right to conduct such defenses, if he chooses to do so." (CCP 2778(f).) In this case, the indemnity provision provides the duty to defend. (UMF no. 8; Cross-Complainant's Evidence, Exhibit H.) The allegations of the Complaint are connected to the subject matter of the subcontract, namely the sandblasting activities. (Complaint, 7.) It is undisputed that Van Acker tendered the defense, and Viking has not accepted tender. (UMF nos. 10, 11.)
In turn, Viking has not met its shifting burden to demonstrate a triable issue of fact. The collateral source rule does not apply in this instance as Van Acker seeks a ruling on the duty to defend, and not reimbursement of attorney fees. This is factually distinguishable from Bramalea California, Inc. v. Reliable Interiors, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 468, 472. The indemnity provision in the subcontract is clear and explicit, and Viking had a duty to defend from tender. "A contractual promise to "defend" another against specified claims clearly connotes an obligation of active responsibility, from the outset, for the promisee's defense against such claims." (Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 553 - 554.) Whether Van Acker was negligent is not at issue in the duty to defend. The duty to defend", as described in the statute, therefore cannot depend on the outcome of that litigation. It follows that, under subdivision 4 of section 2778, claims 'embraced by the indemnity,' as to which the duty to defend is owed, include those which, at the time of tender, allege facts that would give rise to a duty of indemnity." (Id. at 558.) Again, the allegations of the Complaint pertaining to the sandblasting activities triggered the duty to defend based on the language of the indemnity provision. (See Complaint, 7.). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
